artPrompt: A lean grinning red devil gambler mid-leap, one clawed hand flicking a glinting coin skyward in extreme foreshortening, the coin frozen tumbling at the very top of the frame. Carnival-red tents, rigged wheels of fortune and strings of lurid neon blaze behind through smoky black; ember sparks streak past his horns, hot-gold firelight carving his manic face, all-in kinetic menace, rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Double-or-Nothing Devil
text:
Whenever Double-or-Nothing Devil attacks, flip a coin. If you win the flip, it gets +2/+2 until end of turn. If you lose the flip, it gets -1/-1 until end of turn and you grumble.
You may flip again as many times as you dare. Each flip doubles the bonus or the penalty. Greed is a verb here.
flavorText: "One more flip. I can feel it. This one's mine." — last words, several times
